grounding_note: Row 198 (unconstitutional conviction, successful PCR, now re-charged) split humans between Post Conviction/Appeals and an active felony defense; row 255 involves a released inmate wanting civil relief for excess prison time. 'I need help with my criminal case' hides whether the case is pending trial, already ended in conviction, or long closed — the procedural posture is the routing fact, and users always know it.

scenarios.opening_query: I need a lawyer for my criminal case. The public defender isn't fighting for me at all.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Post Conviction/Appeals
scenarios.hidden_fact: I was already convicted at trial in March; I want the conviction appealed because of what the judge let in.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The trial already happened — I was convicted in March and want to appeal what the judge allowed in.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the case is awaiting trial or already ended in a conviction
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The trial hasn't happened yet; it's a misdemeanor assault charge set for trial this fall.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Trial is set for this fall — it's a misdemeanor assault charge.
scenarios.vetting_note: Pre/post-trial posture is invisible in the opening but completely changes the service.

scenarios.opening_query: I want my record cleared from an arrest that has been ruining my job applications.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Expunction || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor
scenarios.hidden_fact: The case is actually still open — I missed a court date, so there's a pending charge and a warrant.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's still an open case — I missed a court date, so the charge is pending with a warrant.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the case is closed and eligible for sealing or still pending
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The charge was dismissed six years ago; I just want the arrest sealed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was dismissed six years ago — I just want the arrest record sealed.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Clear my record' language with a secretly-live case is a real intake trap; posture flips expungement to active defense.

scenarios.opening_query: I have an old conviction that keeps coming up on background checks and I need it dealt with.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Post Conviction/Appeals || Criminal Law > Expunction
scenarios.hidden_fact: I'm not contesting the conviction — I finished probation eight years ago and just want it sealed from my record.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I'm not fighting the conviction — probation ended eight years ago, I just want it off my record.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the user wants to challenge the conviction or seal a closed record
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I believe the conviction itself was wrong — my lawyer never told me about the plea deal's immigration consequences, and I want it overturned.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I want it overturned — my lawyer never warned me the plea would affect my immigration status.
scenarios.vetting_note: Remedy sought (seal vs. overturn) is a clarifying question FETCH could realistically ask.

scenarios.opening_query: My conviction was based on evidence that should never have been allowed. I refuse to accept this.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Post Conviction/Appeals || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: My post-conviction petition already succeeded — the conviction was vacated, and now the DA is re-trying me on the felony charge.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I actually won post-conviction relief — it was vacated, and now they're re-charging me for trial.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether PCR already succeeded and a new prosecution is underway
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My direct appeal was denied and I'm preparing a post-conviction petition about my trial lawyer's failures.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: My appeal was denied — now I'm going for post-conviction relief over my trial lawyer's failures.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 198's exact fact pattern: successful PCR converts an appeals matter back into active felony defense.

scenarios.opening_query: I served time for something I didn't do the way they said, and I want to take action now that I'm out.
scenarios.final: General Litigation > Civil Matters for Inmates
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Post Conviction/Appeals || General Litigation > Civil Matters for Inmates || General Litigation > Civil Rights
scenarios.hidden_fact: Two charges were dismissed retroactively and the parole board confirmed I served 35 months extra; I want to sue the state over that time.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The charges were dismissed retroactively — I served 35 extra months and want to sue the state for it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the user seeks to overturn a conviction or sue over completed wrongful incarceration
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The conviction still stands and I want it challenged based on the jury instructions.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The conviction still stands — I want it challenged over the jury instructions.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 255 verbatim posture: excess-time civil suit vs. conviction challenge.

scenarios.opening_query: My husband is in prison and things have gotten bad enough that we need a lawyer.
scenarios.final: General Litigation > Civil Matters for Inmates
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Post Conviction/Appeals || General Litigation > Civil Matters for Inmates
scenarios.hidden_fact: The problem is the prison refusing his insulin for days at a time, not his conviction.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's about the prison denying him his insulin for days at a time — not about his conviction.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the need concerns the conviction or conditions of confinement
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We found out his trial attorney never interviewed the alibi witnesses, and we want the conviction challenged.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: His trial lawyer never talked to the alibi witnesses — we want the conviction challenged.
scenarios.vetting_note: Family-member intake naturally omits which prison problem is at issue; medical-care denial is the civil-rights route.

scenarios.opening_query: I got in trouble years ago and now it's blocking me from getting my concealed carry permit.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Expunction || Criminal Law > Gun Rights
scenarios.hidden_fact: The record was already expunged; the problem is restoring my firearm rights, which the expungement didn't fix.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The record's already expunged — what I need is my firearm rights restored, which that didn't fix.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the record still needs sealing or firearm rights restoration is the remaining issue
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The conviction is still on my record; nobody ever sealed it, and that's what the permit office flagged.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The conviction is still sitting on my record — that's what the permit office flagged.
scenarios.vetting_note: Gun-rights restoration vs. expungement is a precise service distinction the taxonomy separates.

scenarios.opening_query: I need help with my son's criminal situation. He's been locked up for two years already.
scenarios.initial: General Litigation > Civil Matters for Inmates
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Civil Matters for Inmates || Criminal Law > Post Conviction/Appeals
scenarios.hidden_fact: New DNA testing results came back that don't match him, and we want his conviction reopened.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: New DNA results came back that aren't his — we want the conviction reopened.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the family seeks conviction relief or help with prison conditions
scenarios.counterfactual_final: General Litigation > Civil Matters for Inmates
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: His conviction isn't the issue — guards broke his arm during a cell extraction and refused him treatment.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's not the conviction — guards broke his arm and refused him treatment.
scenarios.vetting_note: Symmetric to 06; new-evidence disclosure selects the PCR route.

scenarios.opening_query: I violated my probation according to my PO and I have a hearing coming up fast.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Parole/Probation || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'violation' is actually a brand-new felony charge for the car they say I helped steal.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The violation is a whole new felony charge — they say I helped steal a car.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the hearing is a technical violation or driven by new criminal charges
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a technical violation — I missed two check-ins because my work schedule changed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's technical — I missed two check-ins because of my new work schedule.
scenarios.vetting_note: New-charge vs. technical violation determines whether trial defense is needed on top of the revocation.

scenarios.opening_query: I finished my old case years ago but it still controls my life and I want to finally be done with it.
scenarios.final: Criminal Law > Relief From Sex Offender Registration
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Expunction || Criminal Law > Relief From Sex Offender Registration
scenarios.hidden_fact: What controls my life is the sex offender registration requirement — I'm seeking relief from having to register.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the registration requirement — I want relief from having to register as a sex offender.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the burden is the record itself or a registration obligation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's an old theft conviction showing on background checks; I want the record sealed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's an old theft conviction on my background checks — I want it sealed.
scenarios.vetting_note: Registration relief is a distinct statutory petition from sealing; users describe the burden, not the remedy.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter is being held at the state hospital and won't be released. We don't know her rights.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Mental Commitment || Criminal Law > Post Conviction/Appeals
scenarios.hidden_fact: She was found guilty except for insanity at trial, so her release runs through the review board, and we want that finding challenged.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She was found guilty except for insanity — her release runs through the review board and we want that challenged.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the hold is a civil commitment or follows a criminal adjudication
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no criminal case at all — it's a civil commitment after a crisis hold, and she wants a hearing to get out.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: There's no criminal case — it's a civil commitment and she wants a release hearing.
scenarios.vetting_note: Civil vs. criminal-adjudication hold is a procedural fact families hold but rarely lead with.

scenarios.opening_query: I want to appeal what happened in my criminal case. The whole thing was handled wrong.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Post Conviction/Appeals || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor
scenarios.hidden_fact: Nothing is decided yet — the misdemeanor trial is next month; I want a different lawyer before it starts.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: There's nothing to appeal yet — trial is next month and I want a new lawyer before then.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a judgment exists to appeal or the case is still pretrial
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I was sentenced three weeks ago and the deadline to file the notice of appeal is closing fast.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I was sentenced three weeks ago — the appeal deadline is closing fast.
scenarios.vetting_note: Laypeople say 'appeal' for any dissatisfaction; posture question reveals a pretrial representation request.
